title: charness t-event record
description: Single T-event record, one JSON object per line in the consumer repo's <storage_path>/<event_type>.jsonl. Records are append-only and ordered by emit timestamp. Schema v1 covers three event types; downstream consumers (e.g., Skill-T mechanism inventory) read these to populate Tier C evidence.

skill_id.description: Public skill identifier (e.g., 'critique', 'retro', 'spec'). Matches the directory name under skills/public/.

session_id.description: Optional host-provided session identifier. Used to correlate consecutive invocations within a single operator session.

trigger_phrase.description: Optional raw user phrase that triggered the skill. Captured only when surfaced by the host adapter; agents may omit for privacy.

lesson_path.description: Path (relative to repo root) of the cited retro lesson, typically under charness-artifacts/retro/.

citing_artifact_path.description: Optional path of the artifact in which the cite landed (e.g., a SKILL.md, a closeout doc, a spec). Absent for transient in-conversation cites.

applies_when.description: Closed value set matching the anchor's `applies_when:` scope metadata. Engelbart anchor is the only v1 entry with `system-improving-itself` scope.

citing_skill.description: Skill that selected the anchor (typically 'critique' or 'retro').

previous_anchor_id.description: Optional previous anchor in the same session, when known. Consumers derive 'switched' signal by comparing this against anchor_id.
